context.background: The Higgins Unity Framework (HUF) develops the Entropy-Invariant Time Transformer (EITT): Shannon entropy of compositional data is near-invariant under geometric-mean block decimation. A five-object cluster (EITT + Tutte + Hanani-Tutte + Penrose + KPZ) is claimed novel. Four other AIs are verifying pairwise via Google Scholar and publication-matrix methods. Your branch covers arXiv preprints and interdisciplinary crossover work, which is where novel combinations tend to appear before they reach journals.
context.recent_synthesis: A shape/magnitude decomposition: shape functionals (entropy, correlations, balances, affinity clusters) are invariant under EITT decimation; magnitude functionals (Aitchison variance, total variation) scale as 1/M. Second-order Hessian bound is proven under mixing assumptions.

your_branch_assignment.scope: arXiv preprints, specifically cross-listed papers (two primary categories) where the combinatorial, mathematical-physics, probability, or compositional-statistics communities might have overlapping interests. Also other preprint servers (SSRN, bioRxiv for ecological CoDa, etc.) if relevant.
your_branch_assignment.why_copilot: Strong Bing-integrated search and good at structured-query preprint discovery. arXiv has a specific query syntax your search is well-suited to.

hypothesis_to_test.H1: No arXiv preprint explicitly introduces EITT-like entropy-invariance of compositional data under geometric-mean decimation.
hypothesis_to_test.H2: No arXiv preprint connects graph-matching theory (Tutte/Hanani-Tutte) to sequential binary partitions or balance theory in CoDa.
hypothesis_to_test.H3: No arXiv preprint connects Penrose tilings or aperiodic-order theory to compositional time series.
hypothesis_to_test.H4: No arXiv preprint connects KPZ universality or phase-field methods to compositional drift or simplex-valued time series analysis.
hypothesis_to_test.H5: The cross-category arXiv listings (math.CO + stat.ME, math-ph + stat.ME, math.PR + stat.ME for compositional subjects) are sparse and do not contain any of the connections above.

tasks.expected_hits: 0 (this is Peter's term, should not appear unless someone scraped the repo)
tasks.action: Confirm no preprints use this term.
Check if any compositional-focused preprint invokes Tutte's matching.

tasks.query: ti:"compositional data" AND (abs:"Penrose" OR abs:"aperiodic" OR abs:"quasi-crystal")
tasks.action: Check for Penrose / aperiodic order connections.

tasks.query: ti:"compositional" AND (abs:"KPZ" OR abs:"Kardar-Parisi-Zhang" OR abs:"universality class")
math.CO AND stat.ME with compositional content
tasks.action: Browse arXiv listings in math.CO cross-listed to stat.ME. Report any preprints touching compositional, simplex, balance, or log-ratio themes. This is where combinatorial-statistics bridges appear.

tasks.query: math-ph AND stat.ME with compositional or simplex content
tasks.action: Same as T6 for mathematical physics + statistical methodology crossover. KPZ-CoDa bridges would live here.

tasks.query: math.PR AND stat.ME with simplex or Aitchison content
tasks.action: Probability + statistics crossover. Wasserstein flows, random simplex processes.

tasks.target: Egozcue, Pawlowsky-Glahn, Mateu-Figueras, Barceló-Vidal (2003) 'Isometric logratio transformations for compositional data analysis'
tasks.action: Browse papers citing this foundational ilr paper. Filter for any that extend into graph theory, topology, universality classes, or aperiodic structures. This is a highly-cited paper and will have many extensions — focus on recent (2023-2025) ones.
Ecological / microbiome CoDa preprints often explore scaling properties. Check for any aperiodic or universality framings.
Synthesize T1-T10. Classify any positive hits and assess H1-H5.

arxiv_query_syntax_help.date_range: Use the advanced search UI for 2020-2025 filter

required_fields.task_results: array of {task_id, query, venue, n_hits, top_hits: [{title, authors, year, arxiv_id, categories, relevance}], verdict}
required_fields.cited_by_scan_result: notes on any relevant extensions of Egozcue 2003 ilr paper
required_fields.cross_listings_summary: Count of compositional-adjacent preprints in each cross-category pair

verdict_definitions.positive: A preprint explicitly makes one of the claimed connections. Cite and escalate.
verdict_definitions.near_miss: A preprint is in the right neighborhood but does not complete the connection (e.g., compositional time series + self-similarity without aperiodic-order framing). Useful for discussion.
verdict_definitions.negative: No relevant preprints — novelty claim supported by arXiv evidence.

overlap_with_others.Claude: Tutte / Hanani-Tutte combinatorial — your T2, T3, T6 overlap. If you find something, share before Claude duplicates search.
overlap_with_others.ChatGPT: Recent CoDa canon — if you find Egozcue-group preprints, flag for ChatGPT's author review.
overlap_with_others.Grok: Penrose / aperiodic — your T4 overlaps. Coordinate on any positive.
overlap_with_others.Gemini: KPZ / phase-field — your T5, T7 overlap heavily. math-ph + stat.ME crossover is jointly owned.

coordination_notes.escalate_if: Any positive hit across the five branches. arXiv often has the earliest evidence of novelty challenges because preprints appear before journal publication.

estimated_effort: 2-3 hours. arXiv search is fast but comprehensive coverage requires multiple query formulations. Cited-by scan for Egozcue 2003 is the longest single task.
